page: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is provisioned with 4 dedicated virtual CPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The underlying hardware utilizes AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage. The instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance persistent disks. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:16, this virtual machine represents a memory-optimized hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory footprints relative to compute power, avoiding the expense of provisioning additional unused vCPUs. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by AMD processors and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are provided. Designed for memory-heavy operations without excessive compute overhead, this server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caches, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database workloads.
